follows the Uniswap V2 design.  Key properties:         - MINIMUM_LIQUIDITY (1000) is permanently burned on the first deposit to prevent           first-depositor inflation attacks.         - The 0.3% fee stays in the pool, gradually increasing k and rewarding LP holders.         - All external state-changing functions are protected by ReentrancyGuard.         - SafeERC20 is used for every token interaction.","errors":{"ReentrancyGuardReentrantCall()":[{"details":"Unauthorized reentrant call."}],"SafeERC20FailedOperation(address)":[{"details":"An operation with an ERC-20 token failed."}]},"kind":"dev","methods":{"addLiquidity(uint256,uint256,uint256,uint256,address,uint256)":{"details":"On the very first deposit, the amounts set the price. addLiquidity(uint256,uint256,uint256,uin.details: On the very first deposit, the amounts set the price.  On subsequent deposits         the optimal amounts are calculated to maintain the current ratio.

params.amountADesired: Maximum token A the caller is willing to deposit
params.amountBDesired: Maximum token B the caller is willing to deposit
params.deadline: Unix timestamp after which the transaction reverts

params._tokenA: Address of the first token in the pair
params._tokenB: Address of the second token in the pair

returns.priceAinB: How many TOKEN_B units per 1e18 TOKEN_A
returns.priceBinA: How many TOKEN_A units per 1e18 TOKEN_B

params.amountAMin: Minimum token A to receive (slippage guard)
params.amountBMin: Minimum token B to receive (slippage guard)
params.deadline: Unix timestamp after which the transaction reverts
params.lpAmount: Amount of LP tokens to burn

params.deadline: Unix timestamp after which the transaction reverts
params.tokenIn: Address of the input token (must be TOKEN_A or TOKEN_B)

addLiquidity(uint256,uint256,uint256,uin.notice: Adds liquidity to the pool and mints LP tokens.

getAmountOut(address,uint256).notice: Returns the expected output for a given input without executing a swap.

removeLiquidity(uint256,uint256,uint256,.notice: Burns LP tokens and returns the proportional share of pool reserves.

swap(address,uint256,uint256,address,uin.notice: Swaps an exact amount of `tokenIn` for as many `tokenOut` as possible.
